title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The local council is said to be reconsidering its decision.
questions.explanation: Tests the impersonal passive construction 'It is said that'.
questions.secondSentence: It (1) _________ the local council is reconsidering its decision.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll take you to the station,’ said my brother.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs followed by a to-infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: If I had known the truth, I wouldn't have been so angry.
questions.explanation: Tests participle clauses used to replace conditional 'if' structures.
questions.secondSentence: Not (1) _________ the truth, I wouldn't have been so angry.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure he didn't do it on purpose.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative deduction in the past.

questions.firstSentence: He had never before been so happy.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with the negative expression 'At no time'.

questions.firstSentence: It is unlikely that the weather will improve tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Tests probability structures with 'little chance/prospect of'.
questions.secondSentence: There is (1) _________ the weather improving tomorrow.
